mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nominal retail sales (RSAFS) signals resilient consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC remains below 0.03 or the fraction of positive 20-day returns following RSAFS increases is not significantly above 50% (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 5.5%, the tightening of financial conditions reduces equity valuations via higher discount rates and increases recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If in at least 60% of triggered instances 20-day forward returns are positive, or if the average excess return relative to no-trigger periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment and decline in housing starts signals weakening labor markets and fading consumer confidence, which depresses economic activity and equity prices over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.2pp in 3 months and HOUST declines > 5% in 3 months
failure_condition: Rank IC over the combined trigger condition is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) or the hit rate of negative returns in triggered instances is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a slack labor market (unemployment above 5%), rising nonfarm payrolls signals improved employment and income, which boosts aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns. The effect reverses when the labor market is tight.
failure_condition: Within the regime, the correlation between PAYEMS changes and subsequent 20-day return is not significantly positive (Rank IC p > 0.10), or the positive return hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output, which signals economic strength and supports short-term equity price gains over the following week.
failure_condition: Over any rolling 1-year window, the proportion of positive 5-day forward returns following INDPRO increases does not exceed 50% by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3%, persistent price pressures lead to tighter monetary expectations and reduced equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next three months.
failure_condition: If at least 50% of triggered instances show positive forward returns, or if the median forward return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (signed-rank test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising short-term interest rates and elevated inflation signals deliberate monetary tightening, which compresses valuations through both higher discount rates and lower expected future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return in the joint trigger state is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the combined Rank IC is below -0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 3%), rising retail sales indicate strong consumer demand without the drag of tight credit, boosting corporate profits and equity prices over the following month. When rates are high the relationship weakens or reverses.
failure_condition: Within the regime, the Rank IC between RSAFS changes and 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10), or the positive return hit rate falls below 55%.
